language.description: Query dialect. Known values: 'kanecta' (native filter/search expression — the same DSL used in pipeline runIf, forEach.filter, and loopBack.if conditions; saveable and reusable as a query item), 'sql' (direct SELECT statement against the SQLite or Postgres adapter index — full join, aggregate, and window-function power; column names come from the items table and derived payload tables), 'graph' (Cypher query against the AGE graph projection — requires AGE to be enabled on the workspace; returns UUIDs that the adapter then resolves to full items). Open string — new dialects do not require a spec change; the adapter resolves dialect names to execution strategies.

expression.description: The query body. For 'kanecta': a filter/search expression, e.g. 'type = "task" AND payload.status = "open" AND payload.assignee = {{params.assignee}}'. For 'sql': a SELECT statement, e.g. 'SELECT i.* FROM items i JOIN items t ON t.id = i.payload->>''typeId'' WHERE t.value = ''task'' AND i.payload->>''status'' = {{params.status}}'. For 'graph': a Cypher MATCH/RETURN statement. May contain {{params.name}} placeholders substituted at execution time. Placeholders in SQL are injected as parameterised values (never string-interpolated) to prevent injection.

params.description: Named parameters that make this query reusable. Each parameter is substituted into {{params.name}} placeholders in expression at execution time. The adapter validates supplied values against the declared type before execution. Studio generates an input form from this definition when a user runs the query interactively. Null or empty array means no parameters — the query is fixed.

name.description: Parameter name. Referenced in expression as {{params.name}}. Must be unique within the params array.

type.description: Parameter type. Used for validation before execution and for Studio input form generation (uuid renders a UUID picker, date renders a date input, etc.).

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this parameter controls. Shown as a label in the Studio input form.

default.description: Default value used when the parameter is not supplied at execution time. Must match the declared type. Null means the parameter is required — the adapter rejects execution if the parameter is missing and has no default.

returnType.description: UUID of the item type this query returns, when results map cleanly to a single known type (e.g. the UUID of the 'task' type item for a query that filters tasks). Studio uses this to render the grid with type-specific column definitions and to offer type-aware actions on result rows. Null when the query produces a derived result set — a join, aggregate, or cross-type projection — that does not correspond to a single item type. In the null case Studio infers column schema from the result set. Informational — the adapter does not validate result rows against this type at execution time.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this query does, when to use it, and any important caveats (e.g. 'Slow on large datasets — add an index on payload.status before running in production'). Shown alongside the query name in Studio and in kanecta_run_query output.

sqlSchema: -- Built-in type: adapter handles storage natively.
